title: ranunculus defectus
intro: Ranunculus defectus, also known as the lesser celandine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus defectus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health concerns.
